states._comment: **2026-05-25 fan-out (F4/F5):** opener replaced with the canonical Cula first-meeting greeting (name + self-id + period per AGENTS.md §First-meeting introductions); previous bare 'Good morning' violated the rule. Tail line 'I will take that as a fact and find Mr. Murrow.' inlined after 'Six weeks. Understood.' — the second beat was missing in pig.json and the original flow jumped to 'Anyway. I'm digressing.' without registering Cula's pivot to Murrow. Both lifted from cula.json::cula_b3_pig_first_encounter and cula_b3_pig_rent_reaction; those states queued for deletion from cula.json.
lines: It would appear that we are...

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mr. Pig. Cula. The reception sent me back.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: I gather there is a great deal happening this morning.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Would you like to start with the firm, the client, or the deadline?

lines: Dr. A. Cula! Welcome. You arrive during, well... a moment.
Let me assure you first: the firm is not bankrupt. Bankruptcy is for firms whose paperwork has caught up with them. We are luckier than that, in a manner of speaking.
The rent is six weeks overdue.
Six weeks under the surface. The landlord knocks. Politely. The polite knock is somehow worse than a loud one.
It would appear that this can continue indefinitely, on momentum, which I am told is a recognised force in physics and almost recognised in accounting.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: There is also the printer lease, which has lapsed, and which I have been renewing by telephone every Thursday, and which the leasing company has stopped answering the telephone about, which is new.

lines: So. We carry on. Less every morning, but recognisably ourselves.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: I will take that as a fact and find Mr. Murrow.

lines: Anyway. I'm digressing. This is your first day, and we need you to start immediately. We have a case to take care of.
Mr. Murrow has the folder. Mr. Murrow knows the— ask Mr. Murrow.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: Right. Right. Yes. Welcome to Pig & Swine.

states._comment: **2026-05-25 fan-out (F4/F5):** Beat 3 dwell-prompt picker for Mr. Pig. Scaffolded from cula.json::cula_b3_pig_dwell_options. Cula prompt is the picker itself (state-level speaker:'cula'); reply states gate on chapter1.state_choice and clear it on dismiss. Ordered AFTER pig_first_meeting and BEFORE post_meeting_pre_murrow so dwells fire while Cula is still in the room with Pig pre-Murrow.
states.speaker: cula
lines: [A pause. Pig is still here; ask one more thing before going to find Mr. Murrow.]

choices.text: Mr. Pig. The case itself. Could you give me the shape of it?
Before I go, Mr. Pig. Are you all right? Today aside.
And Mr. Swine. Is he expected back this week, or is he travelling?

Cula's prompt + TODO for Pig's deflection (Piotr to author per 'you author, I wire' decision 2026-05-25).

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mr. Pig. The case itself. Could you give me the shape of it?

lines: [TODO 2026-05-25: Piotr to author Pig's deflection to Murrow. Voice: panicked-while-formal, escalating then re-routing. Pig knows the case exists; he should not be able to describe its actual procedural shape. End on pointing Cula at Mr. Murrow with dignity intact.]

states._comment: **2026-05-25 fan-out:** Beat 3 dwell — *Are you okay?* Per cula.json::cula_b3_pig_dwell_okay_reply _comment: 'pierces the panic for one beat; expected reply is a brief admission of sleeplessness per the beat sheet. Exposes Pig's interior range.' Cula's prompt + TODO for Pig's reply (Piotr to author).

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Before I go, Mr. Pig. Are you all right? Today aside.

lines: [TODO 2026-05-25: Piotr to author Pig's brief admission. Voice: formal-while-cracking. One unguarded sentence about sleeplessness or the rent, then immediate recomposition with a formal redirect. Sets up the Ch5 sincere line by establishing Pig's interior range here.]

states._comment: **2026-05-25 fan-out:** Beat 3 dwell — *Where is Mr. Swine?* Per cula.json::cula_b3_pig_dwell_swine_reply _comment: 'clarifying question that plants Beat 14 / Chapter 4 Mr. Swine thread.' Cula's prompt + TODO for Pig's evasion-or-half-truth (Piotr to author).

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And Mr. Swine. Is he expected back this week, or is he travelling?

lines: [TODO 2026-05-25: Piotr to author Pig's reply about Swine's absence. Voice: airy-evasive on Swine's whereabouts; Pig has incomplete information himself. Plant the Beat 14 postcard (Japan / Sapporo per chapter_1.md) and the Ch4 return without naming either directly.]
Yes. Mr. Murrow. As I said. Apologies for the noise. Please don't forget to bill this conversation under "Business Development".

states._comment: **2026-05-25 critique F11:** maritime metaphor ('crawled below deck') cut in service of letting Ch5 sincere-Pig land. Replaced with a Hrabal-rhythm wandering sentence that lands on the printer-lease — Pig's other established Ch1 anxiety. The tic now appears once in Ch1 (pig_first_meeting), in idle_flavor (ambient between scenes), and nowhere else.
lines: The procedural binder is in the archive. Or it was, when I last saw it, which I want to say was Tuesday — Tuesday was also when I last saw the printer-lease renewal, and only one of those two things can still be true.
The procedural binder. Good. Half the picture is better than none, though in court the other half tends to matter.

states._comment: V1.4 coffee reaction — S-grade. Voice draft from minigames.txt §Character reactions §Mr. Pig Perfect ('Can we sell this? No, wait, can we bill it?'), Taste-Standard pass adds the opening address per Pig's existing exclamatory cadence.
lines: Dr. A. Cula! Can we sell this? No — better — can we bill it?

states._comment: V1.4 coffee reaction — D/F-grade. Voice draft from minigames.txt §Character reactions §Mr. Pig Bad ('That coffee smells like our accounts.'). Reads alongside the firm's existing six-weeks-underwater crisis joke.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. That coffee smells like our accounts.

states._comment: 2026-05-19 critique F9: previously-missing Beat 13 celebration speech that cula_b13_pig_celebration_response in cula.json was authored to react to. Firm-first / client-second ordering is load-bearing — the player should register that this firm celebrates its own survival before the client's. Maritime tic recedes (sincere undercut beat per style_canon §Mr. Pig arc rule; not the Ch5 fully-sincere moment, but the first hairline crack in the panic frame). once:true uses SAVE_VERSION 12 declarative fire-once (dialogue_states_seen) — no manual flag needed. Ordered before has_binder_and_memo so this state wins on Beat 13. **2026-05-25 fan-out:** Cula's response inlined from cula.json::cula_b13_pig_celebration_response (queued for deletion).
lines: Dr. A. Cula! We have survived another impossible thing.
The firm carries on. The client — yes, the client — also carries on. Both, mercifully, in that order.
I would offer a toast, but the only glasses in this office are the ones Mr. Murrow reads with.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Temporarily saved is still saved, Mr. Pig. I am learning that this office measures success in survivable intervals.

lines: The binder and the memo. I would say I am proud, but pride requires a moment of stillness and we do not currently have one.

idle_flavor: For the first time today, the ship is not actively sinking. It is merely making exploratory gurgling noises.
Has anyone seen my optimism? It was here this morning.
We cannot sail into court with a hole in the hull and optimism as our only oar.
